sources.title: Sell-Side Research Leadership and Org Design
claims: The Global Head of Research sets the overall strategy, quality standards, and business model for the research organization.
Goldman Sachs' Jan Hatzius has served as Chief Economist and Head of Global Investment Research since 2020, evaluating analysts on 'client votes and stock picking'.
JPMorgan's Joyce Chang sits on the Corporate & Investment Bank Management Committee and oversees 920 research professionals across 26 countries covering 3,600 stocks and 1,100 credits.
Barclays appointed Brad Rogoff, previously head of FICC Research, as Global Head of Research in October 2024, reflecting growing cross-asset research integration.

sources.title: Company Coverage Analyst in Sell-Side Research
claims: Makes final investment recommendations and target prices for 10-20 companies in a sector.
Covers 8-12 large-cap names or 15-20 names when including mid- and small-cap stocks.
Core value lies in relationship networks with executives and institutional investors rather than modeling alone.
In sell-side research, Vice President, Director, and Managing Director professionals are all externally called 'Analyst.'
Promotion to Analyst typically takes 2-5 years; top-ranked analysts can earn over $1 million annually.

sources.title: Research Associate in Sell-Side Research
claims: Research Associates support senior analysts through financial model updates, data collection and verification, draft report writing, and conference call notes.
Their workload is roughly split as 30% model updates, 25% data collection/verification, 25% report drafting, 10% conference call notes, and 10% ad-hoc requests.
Associates hand off model spreadsheets, data packs, and draft sections to VP/MD analysts.
Junior professional with 0-4 years of experience supporting senior analysts.
Each associate covers about 7-10 names; teams usually consist of one senior analyst and 2-3 associates.
Work is roughly 70% writing and 30% modeling, though this varies widely by senior analyst style.
Entry-level associates build three-statement model basics, DCF sub-sheets, and comps data; senior associates independently build models and write drafts.
During earnings season, associates work 70-80 hours per week across four quarterly peaks.
Typical U.S. base salary is $125,000-$200,000 plus bonus.

sources.title: Equity Strategist in Sell-Side Research
claims: Sets the direction for global equity markets, index targets, and sector allocation.
Peter Oppenheimer served as Goldman Sachs Chief Global Equity Strategist for more than 28 years and co-led Global Economics, Commodities and Strategy research.
Morgan Stanley's Global Equity Allocation team has used a top-down approach since 1986 to set regional, country, sector, and industry overweights and underweights.
The role typically requires PhD-level quantitative capability combined with market sense and around 15 or more years of experience.
Key deliverables include the Annual Strategy Outlook, Global Equity Outlook, Sector Rotation Matrix, and monthly ranking model.

sources.title: Macro Economist and Strategist
claims: The Chief Economist focuses on macroeconomic forecasting, policy analysis, and asset allocation strategy.
At most investment banks, the Chief Economist supervises only the macro research division, whereas the Global Head of Research covers all asset classes.
Goldman Sachs is an exception where Jan Hatzius combines the Chief Economist and Global Head of GIR roles.
Holds final responsibility for macroeconomic forecasts including GDP, inflation, interest rates, and employment.
Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid concurrently heads Global Macro and Thematic Research, Corporate Bank Research, and Credit Strategy.
Jim Reid published the 'Early Morning Reid' report daily for 18 years, building Deutsche Bank Research's most-subscribed publication.
The role typically requires a PhD in economics and 15 or more years of experience, with central-bank and policy networks as key assets.
Estimated salary range is $125,000-$175,000.

sources.title: Fixed Income and Credit Research
claims: Analyzes investment-grade corporate bonds, credit risk, financial health, and credit spreads.
JP Morgan's Global Research Credit Strategy Analyst contributes to top-down U.S. investment-grade credit views using data-driven research and modeling.
A typical day starts at 6:45 a.m., includes a Bloomberg news scan, morning desk call, earnings calls, detailed note writing, and compliance review.
Earnings flash notes must be published within 60-90 minutes after results.
Produces credit snapshots, issuer ratings, investment-grade relative-value matrices, and earnings flash notes.

sources.title: Editorial and Publishing Operations
claims: Research Editors check linguistic accuracy, logical consistency, and structural clarity of analyst drafts.
They do not change the analyst's investment view but ensure it is conveyed accurately and clearly.
Without editorial support, analysts waste time on layout and language issues instead of focusing on research and market interpretation.

sources.title: Research Sales and Distribution
claims: Research Salespersons amplify research reports through personal client outreach, build and maintain relationships with new funds, and repackage research for client-specific strategies.
They attend daily morning calls to identify high-impact research and pitch ideas to institutional investors.
First-year sales and trading associates receive total compensation of approximately $240,000-$270,000.

sources.title: Research Compliance and Information Barriers
claims: RBC's Research Compliance Oversight role supervises information barriers, operates the control room, conducts pre-publication review, manages MNPI, prepares regulatory inquiry responses, and delivers compliance training.
Required qualifications include Series 7, 2+ years of financial services experience, proficiency in FINRA Rule 2241 and Regulation M, control room experience, and a law degree is preferred.
The role reports to the Chief Compliance Officer and legal department and maintains functional independence from the research head.

sources.title: ESG and Thematic Research
claims: JPMorgan Sustainable Investing Research Analysts research financially material ESG issues, develop analytical tools, write reports, and collaborate with global portfolio managers.
They integrate ESG factors into financial forecasts and valuation using DCF discount-rate adjustments, multiple adjustments, and cash-flow revisions.
The role spends roughly 25-30% of time on ESG data and scoring and 25-30% on integrating ESG into financial models.

sources.title: Generic Finance Career Forum Thread
claims: Forum discussions may contain anecdotes but lack structured role analysis.

sources.title: Regional Head Of Research
claims: Regional Heads manage coverage priorities, analyst teams, and local client service within a specific region such as Americas, EMEA, or APAC.
Nomura's APAC Equity Research uses a Joint-Head structure, with CW Chung leading Korea/pan-Asia Tech and Masataka Kunugimoto leading Japan/Asia-Pacific Auto.
Regional Heads translate global strategy into local coverage plans and act as intermediaries between the Global Head and Sector Heads.

sources.title: Sector Head
claims: Sector Heads are responsible for research quality, coverage strategy, and mentoring analysts within a specific industry sector.
They act as the operational 'combat commander' of the research organization and have authority to decide which companies are covered.
Coverage decisions by Sector Heads frequently involve negotiation or conflict with traders.
Oversees all coverage analysts within an industry sector and is responsible for sector-wide research direction and quality.
Typically holds a Managing Director title with 15 or more years of experience.
Sets sector overweights and underweights, manages the team, maintains client relationships, and decides new coverage.
Sector-specialist funds have recorded 340-940 basis points higher IRR than generalist funds.
Buy-side voters value in-depth industry knowledge and accessibility/responsiveness most.

sources.title: Research Coo
claims: The Research COO supervises day-to-day operations, process improvement, and infrastructure for the research organization.
Deutsche Bank maintains a VP-level Head of Research Business Operations overseeing hundreds of equity analysts, economists, and fixed income strategists.
This role prioritizes operational efficiency and change management over research experience, with salaries around £43,200-£72,000.

sources.title: Equity Research Coverage Analyst
claims: MD-level Coverage Analysts typically cover 10-15 large companies, build financial models, issue Buy/Hold/Sell recommendations, and meet with C-suite and IR teams.
VP-level Research Analysts usually cover 8-12 mid-cap companies and handle modeling, report writing, and investor inquiries.
Small/Mid-Cap Analysts cover 15-20 companies and produce initiation reports of 50-100+ pages.

sources.title: Fixed Income Credit Research
claims: Credit Research Analysts analyze corporate bond issuers' credit risk and default probability, producing issuer ratings, credit snapshots, IG/HY RV matrices, and daily credit notes.
Rates Strategists provide interest-rate market forecasts, yield curve analysis, duration strategies, and central bank previews.
The Head of Fixed Income Research manages overall fixed income research strategy and coordinates rates and credit teams.

sources.title: Quantitative Research
claims: Quantitative Research develops factor models, algorithmic strategies, and derivative pricing models for systematic investors and trading desks.
Goldman Sachs Quantitative Investment Strategies accounts for 27% of total staff and provides systematic strategies to research clients.
Quant roles operate on a dual track: Trading-aligned Quants focus on P&L-adjacent revenue generation, while Research-aligned Quants support analysis and model development.

sources.title: Esg Sustainability Research
claims: ESG Research has a dual mission of investment analysis and regulatory response, covering EU Taxonomy, SFDR, and CSRD.
The three-layer ESG model consists of a centralized ESG data team, sector-embedded ESG integration, and client-facing ESG advisory.
ESG Sector Analysts integrate ESG risk assessments and assumptions into sector-specific company models.

sources.title: Research Production Editorial
claims: Research Editors review language, structure, and investment-logic consistency of research reports.
Supervisory Analysts provide final pre-publication approval for compliance and data accuracy; FINRA Rule 2241 mandates this role for member firms.
Research Production Specialists handle template-based formatting, chart and table insertion, and timely publication of reports.

sources.title: Corporate Access
claims: Corporate Access connects corporate management with institutional investors through non-deal roadshows, field trips, and approximately six conferences per year.
Wells Fargo Corporate Access Analysts earn between $104,000 and $168,000 annually.
MiFID II exempts corporate access for companies below £200 million market capitalization, while ESMA permits payment from Research Payment Accounts.

sources.title: Compliance Regulatory Review
claims: Research Compliance Officers supervise information barriers, control rooms, pre-approval processes, MNPI management, and training.
FINRA Rule 2241 prohibits investment banking employees from pre-reviewing or approving research reports and bars analyst compensation from being tied to specific investment banking transactions.
Control Room Analysts maintain watch/restricted lists, evaluate material nonpublic information, and document deal-team access permissions.

sources.title: Data Analytics Visualization
claims: Research Data Analysts process alternative data, manage quality, and create visualizations, collaborating with quant researchers, data engineers, and coverage analysts.
UBS Evidence Lab spans more than 55 specialist areas and over 1,000 datasets.
Alternative Data Analysts collect and analyze unstructured data through web scraping, social media analysis, consumer surveys, supply chain monitoring, and machine learning/NLP.

sources.title: Research Distribution Product Management
claims: Research Distribution Managers run the morning call process, track readership metrics, manage Institutional Investor poll strategy, and produce thematic emails.
Research Product Managers schedule morning calls, maintain report best-practice guides, collect client feedback, and develop ranking strategies.
The morning-call pipeline follows a handoff chain from analyst to product management, to equity salesperson, to sales trader, and finally to broker vote evaluation.

sources.title: Regional Equity Strategist
claims: Specializes in the equity market of a specific region such as Europe, Asia, or Japan.
Acts as a translation layer between global strategy and local sector analysts.
Nomura's Yunosuke Ikeda moved from Chief FX Strategist to Chief Equity Strategist, showing FX analysis skills can extend into equity strategy.
Produces regional outlooks, weekly regional notes, and regional sector rankings.

sources.title: Sector Strategist
claims: Advises capital allocation within a specific sector rather than picking individual stocks.
Evaluates relative attractiveness of sub-sectors, for example software versus hardware versus semiconductors within technology.
Partially overlaps with sector analysts but is differentiated by a higher-level allocation perspective.
Produces sector strategy notes, sub-sector rotation matrices, and thematic sector reports.

sources.title: Quantitative Equity Strategist
claims: Generates strategy insights through quantitative models, factor analysis, and portfolio optimization.
Savita Subramanian at Bank of America concurrently heads ESG Research and U.S. Equity & Quantitative Strategy.
Uses tools such as Python, R, and machine learning to develop quantitative models.
Requires a skill set distinct from traditional fundamental strategists.

sources.title: Senior Economist
claims: Specializes in macroeconomic analysis of a specific country or region.
Nomura's Rob Subbaraman leads a 20-person team as Head of Global Macro Research and has recruited buy-side talent such as David Seif as Developed Markets Chief Economist.
Economic analysts produce original written research, develop quantitative models, and interpret high-frequency data releases.
Hiring often occurs through Economics PhD Fellowship programs.

sources.title: Macro Strategist
claims: Translates macroeconomic forecasts into actionable insights for rates and foreign-exchange markets.
Combines policy analysis with quantitative modeling.
Nomura's Yunosuke Ikeda developed the 'Nomura Yen Supply-Demand Index' to capture market themes not visible in raw data.
Deutsche Bank's macro research structure includes separate global heads for Economic Research, Rates Research, and FX Research.
Outputs include asset-allocation frameworks, macro strategy notes, rates/FX outlooks, and cross-asset trade ideas.

sources.title: Rates Strategist
claims: Focuses on government bond markets, yield-curve shape, duration positioning, and central-bank policy market impact.
Translates economists' macro forecasts into market prices rather than forecasting macro variables.
A Barclays U.S. Rates Strategist posting specifies Python/MATLAB proficiency and pays $125,000-$175,000 at the AVP level.
Produces daily rates strategy notes, yield-curve outlooks, central-bank previews and reactions, and week-ahead calendars.

sources.title: Hy Distressed Analyst
claims: Analyzes high-yield bonds and distressed corporate debt.
Skill set overlaps considerably with equity analysts.
Examines capital structure, default probability, and debt-restructuring scenarios.
Morgan Stanley's High Yield team implements autonomous approaches within the Fixed Income division.

sources.title: Fig Structured Credit Analyst
claims: FIG credit analysts specialize in debt of banks, insurers, and other financial institutions, requiring understanding of regulatory capital frameworks.
Structured credit analysts analyze MBS, ABS, and CDO products.
Requires complex quantitative modeling including prepayment models, default correlation, and cash flow waterfalls.

sources.title: Fx Strategist
claims: Analyzes currency values and develops currency strategies.
Core work areas include monetary policy analysis, carry-trade strategies, and macro-fundamental modeling using purchasing-power parity, macro indicators, and positioning data.
Uses Python, R, and EViews for quantitative modeling.
Nomura's Yunosuke Ikeda created the 'Nomura Yen Supply-Demand Index' and moved from FX strategist to Chief Equity Strategist.
Deutsche Bank's George Saravelos serves as Global Head of FX Research.

sources.title: Commodities Strategist
claims: Analyzes supply-demand dynamics and price forecasts for energy, metals, and agricultural commodities.
Goldman Sachs' Jeff Currie served as Global Head of Commodities Research, covering corporate risk management and short- and long-term commodity investment strategy.
Goldman Sachs' 2026 Commodity Views Outlook cites U.S.-China AI and geopolitical competition plus a global energy supply wave as its core scenario.
The role typically prefers a PhD or master's in economics and requires macroeconomic research experience.

sources.title: Precious Metals Analyst
claims: Focuses on gold, silver, and platinum.
Gold has safe-haven asset attributes and tends to have an inverse relationship with real interest rates.
Collaborates frequently with rates strategists.
Tracks central-bank gold reserve changes, ETF flows, and jewelry and industrial demand.

sources.title: Quantitative Researcher
claims: Develops mathematical models, data analysis, statistical modeling, and portfolio optimization.
JP Morgan Quantitative Research collaborates with traders, marketers, and risk managers across products and regions in centralized and embedded forms.
Doctoral program enrollees enter at Associate level, while bachelor's and master's graduates enter at Analyst level.
Credit quantitative researchers develop valuation, risk, P&L, pricing, and market-making algorithms for global spread businesses.
Rates quantitative researchers develop arbitrage-free pricing models for interest-rate derivatives using stochastic processes.

sources.title: Derivatives Strategist
claims: Researches, designs, and implements pricing models for equity options, volatility products, and structured derivatives.
Works more closely with trading desks than any other research role.
Uses stochastic processes, finite-difference methods, and Monte Carlo simulation.
Morgan Stanley's Exotic Derivatives Strats implement quantitative models, pricing methodologies, and risk-management and hedging infrastructure.
Success is tied directly to trading desk P&L and profitability.

sources.title: Asset Allocation Strategist
claims: Develops portfolio construction and risk-management strategies at the intersection of macro, quantitative, and fundamental analysis.
UBS Multi Asset Analysts support macro asset-allocation decisions for multi-asset strategies and funds.
Uses tools such as FactSet, Morningstar, Axioma, and MSCI Barra.
Deutsche Bank's quantitative strategy team identified a non-diversifiable liquidity factor separate from recognized factors such as value, size, and momentum.

sources.title: Index Etf Strategist
claims: Develops rule-based systematic strategies and productizes them for clients.
Goldman Sachs' Systematic Trading Strategies group offers strategies via OTC derivatives, certificates, notes, and funds.
BlackRock's iShares product innovation team supports research, build, and development of new ETFs and performs custom backtesting and scenario analysis.

sources.title: Group Head
claims: Manages two or more related sectors, for example TMT (Technology, Media, Telecom).
Coordinates cross-sector themes and reduces resource duplication.
Adjusts uneven coverage sizes across sectors.

sources.title: Thematic Cross Sector Analyst
claims: Analyzes long-term structural trends that cross traditional sector boundaries, such as AI, climate change, demographics, and geopolitical risk.
Cross-sector analysts integrate macro-level themes with micro-level sector impacts.
Nomura's 'Asia Insights' is jointly authored by economists, strategists, and analysts to combine macro themes with company analysis.

sources.title: Co Coverage Analyst
claims: Operates in a two-person coverage model for mega-cap or heavily traded companies.
The lead analyst makes final decisions while the co-coverage analyst handles detailed modeling and daily monitoring.
Common for mega-cap technology companies and large banks, especially during M&A or high earnings-impact periods.

sources.title: Initiating Analyst
claims: Leads new coverage initiations producing 50-100 page Initiating Coverage Reports.
Report structure follows Executive Summary, Business Overview, Industry Analysis, Financial Analysis, Valuation, and Risk Factors.
Coordinates associates for modeling and data collection and editorial for formatting, acting as project manager.

sources.title: Esg Quant Analyst
claims: GS SUSTAIN develops proprietary ESG scores and datasets for approximately 7,000 companies and delivers them via API.
ESG Quant Analysts perform machine-learning-based scoring, integrate ESG into factor models, and measure portfolio-level ESG risk.
Python and SQL programming skills are essential for this hybrid finance-quant role.

sources.title: Climate Transition Analyst
claims: HSBC's Climate and ESG Research Analyst manages climate and ESG databases, tracks policy, conducts country research, and analyzes regulatory impact.
The role quantifies physical and transition climate risks beyond traditional ESG analysis.
It evaluates how climate transition scenarios affect creditworthiness and stock prices in carbon-intensive sectors.

sources.title: Policy Regulatory Analyst
claims: Policy and Regulatory Analysts monitor regulatory changes such as EU SFDR, CSRD, and EU Taxonomy and analyze legislative impacts on sectors and companies.
Barclays places policy and regulatory career experts directly within its ESG team.
Changes to SFDR Article 9 classification criteria are increasing ESG research precision while causing de-classification of some social-objective funds.

sources.title: Thematic Analyst
claims: Thematic Analysts identify beneficiaries of long-term structural changes and research cross-sector themes.
Barclays Global Thematic Investing Research studies water, robotics, and automation over long horizons while analyzing fund flows.
Citi GPS has dedicated Thematic Analysts covering nature and gender themes.
BNP Paribas Exane elevated Clean Energy Research to a team equal to traditional sector coverage.

sources.title: Esg Organization Models
claims: ESG research functions are organized into three models: Centralized, Embedded, and Hybrid.
Goldman Sachs' GS SUSTAIN is a centralized model; Deutsche Bank uses an embedded sector-analyst model; Barclays uses a hybrid model.
The most sophisticated architecture has three layers: centralized data and methodology, sector-specific ESG integration, and client-facing ESG advisory.
ESG research demand surged after 2018 MiFID II despite overall sell-side research budget cuts, driven by EU Taxonomy, SFDR, and CSRD.

sources.title: Research Data Analyst
claims: Citadel Securities' Research Data Analyst processes, improves quality, and monitors alternative datasets to strengthen alpha signals for systematic trading and research.
The role requires SQL, Tableau, and a STEM degree and collaborates closely with Quantitative Researchers and Data Engineers.
At large investment banks, Research Data Analysts operate in 10-20 person teams as a hub among multiple stakeholders.

sources.title: Research Technologist
claims: Goldman Sachs GIR Engineering uses cloud, mobile, and big-data technologies to build digital investment research strategy and modeling infrastructure.
JPMorgan's Quantitative Research Developer designs and implements trading algorithms in production platforms and integrates AI/ML methodologies into modeling.
The role bridges cutting-edge research and practical application and requires both finance domain knowledge and engineering capability.

sources.title: Research Platform Engineer
claims: Citadel Securities' Research Platform Engineer designs and develops distributed systems for large-scale simulations.
The role primarily uses Go and C++ and requires experience with cloud platforms such as GCP and AWS.
It contributes directly to revenue by building core infrastructure for research alpha generation.

sources.title: Alternative Data Analyst
claims: UBS Evidence Lab Alternative Data Analysts transform structured and unstructured datasets into actionable insights to help investors integrate alternative data into their processes.
They collaborate with researchers, engineers, data scientists, and sales across a global team.
UBS Evidence Lab covers 55+ specialized areas, 1,000+ datasets, and 5,000+ companies, and was ranked #1 in alternative data by Greenwich Associates in 2025.

sources.title: Data Visualization Specialist
claims: Data Visualization Specialists create charts, graphs, and infographics that visually convey data-driven arguments in research reports.
A JPMorgan-Tableau collaboration study found that a one standard-deviation increase in visualization use was associated with a 4.1% improvement in EPS forecast accuracy.
More experienced analysts and analysts at larger brokers tend to use visualization tools less.

sources.title: Research Operations Analyst
claims: Barclays' Research Operations Analyst manages research product production and delivery, supports MiFID II compliance, and improves systems with IT.
The global team is based in London, New York, and Mumbai and handles MiFID II entertainment packages, conflict management, and IT system testing.
Some investment banks appoint a Research Division COO to oversee overall research business operations, budget, and compliance.

sources.title: Production Coordinator
claims: Berenberg's Equity Research Production Coordinator handles electronic distribution, MS Word and PowerPoint formatting, and Excel chart creation.
The role requires knowledge of Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, and InDesign.
JPMorgan's Mumbai-based Production Specialist works night shifts starting between 8pm and 11pm, for up to 3-4 consecutive weeks, to provide 24-hour coverage.

sources.title: Publication Manager
claims: Acuity Knowledge Partners' Research Publishing team provides end-to-end research publishing services including Supervisory Analyst approval, editing, formatting, and distribution.
The team operates a follow-the-sun engagement model and has more than 20 years of experience.
Senior roles such as Preqin's Head of Production require 15+ years of research, analysis, editing, and production experience.

sources.title: Translator Localization Specialist
claims: KL Translations provides equity research translation in more than 100 languages and requires translators to have at least 5 years of equity research translation experience.
Ulatus supported Huatai Securities' English equity report issuance, reducing translation turnaround time by 65%.
AI translation integrated with Document Management Systems and Translation Management Systems accelerates revision tracking and automatic company profile application.

sources.title: Research Distribution Manager
claims: Research Distribution Managers manage the morning call process, decide which analysts participate, guide research report best practices, track leadership metrics, and manage the annual Institutional Investor voting process.
Jefferies' Equity Research Product Management Associate starts work at 5:30am to prepare for the 7:30 Equity Morning Call and produces weekly thematic and stock research emails.
The role functions as the marketing arm of the research department.

sources.title: Client Service Analyst
claims: Research Coordinators manage schedules, travel, and expenses for 4-5 analysts and support logistics for marketing events, non-deal roadshows, and medical conferences.
Institutional Distribution Analysts support consultant relations and institutional sales teams by preparing briefing documents and developing pipeline opportunities.
The salary range for a Client Service Analyst in New York City is $105,000-$115,000.

sources.title: Corporate Access Manager
claims: Corporate Access Managers organize non-deal roadshows, field trips, and investor conferences that connect corporate management with institutional investors.
Wells Fargo's Corporate Access Analyst role pays $104,000-$168,000 and requires Series 7 and 63 licenses.
Academic research indicates corporate access is the area buy-side investors value most from sell-side firms.
ESMA does not classify corporate access as research, but it may be paid from a Research Payment Account.

sources.title: Sales Desk Research Liaison
claims: The Sales Desk Research Liaison acts as a real-time information bridge between analysts and the sales desk.
They coordinate analyst appearances on the morning call, summarize report key points into 30-second client pitches, and relay client feedback back to analysts.
Jefferies' Product Management Associate starts at 5:30am to prepare for the 7:30 morning call.

sources.title: Sales Trader
claims: Sales Traders execute agency trades without taking principal risk.
They pitch short-term execution ideas based on research and support liquidity sourcing for large orders.
They collect market color from the trading desk and relay client execution feedback to the research team.

sources.title: Trading Idea Feedback Coordinator
claims: The Trading Idea Feedback Coordinator injects short-term market dynamics back into research production.
They analyze hedge fund positioning data from Prime Brokerage, track option market event pricing, and report supply-and-demand anomalies.
Their output becomes an input for analyst model updates and report revisions.

sources.title: Research Supervisory Analyst
claims: The Supervisory Analyst is the final compliance checkpoint before public research distribution.

FINRA Series 16 comprises a regulatory side (Series 161, 34 questions plus 16 department-related questions, 72% pass) and a securities valuation side (Series 162, 16 questions plus 34 reasonable-basis questions, 74% pass).
CFA Level I pass holders are exempt from Series 162.

sources.title: Information Barrier Manager
claims: The Information Barrier Manager acts as a pre-approval gatekeeper for communications between research and investment banking.
They manage Watch Lists and Restricted Lists, assess MNPI, document deal team access, and review staff movement after transactions.
They implement controls such as disabling email autocomplete, enabling external distribution popup warnings, and operating pre-trade approval systems.

sources.title: Primary Research Coordinator
claims: Evercore ISI uses proprietary surveys as a core differentiator and was ranked #1 Firm in the Extel All-America Survey for four consecutive years from 2022 to 2025.
Ed Hyman's Macro franchise includes Global Economics, Global Policy, Surveys, China, and Technical Analysis teams.
Primary research provides independent data that is not available from corporate disclosures.

sources.title: Expert Network Coordinator
claims: Expert Network Coordinators source and match experts through platforms such as GLG, Third Bridge, and AlphaSights.
AlphaSights is recognized for speed and scale; Third Bridge differentiates itself through analyst-led interviews and a transcript library.
The SEC closely monitors MNPI leakage through expert networks and requires call tracking, recording, summaries, secondary note review, and trading activity review.

sources.title: Channel Check Analyst
claims: Channel Check Analysts collect independent information through distribution channels that is not provided by the analyzed companies.
They interview customers, distributors, and suppliers to cross-verify corporate financial health.
The SEC does not ban legitimate channel checks; it prohibits only obviously illegal tip-based information gathering.

sources.title: Alternative Data Integration Specialist
claims: Alternative Data Integration Specialists collect and process unstructured data beyond traditional financial data.
UBS Evidence Lab covers 55+ specialized areas, billions of monthly data items, 1,000+ datasets, 5,000+ companies, and operates 1,000+ web-scraping robots.
Data types include geospatial, pricing and trading, social media, and supply chain data.

sources.title: Multimedia Producer
claims: Morgan Stanley Research Creative Suite converts approximately 70,000 annual research reports into dynamic audio, video, and editorial marketing assets.
The role uses Adobe Premiere, After Effects, and Audition.
Major sell-side podcasts include J.P. Morgan's Making Sense, Deutsche Bank's Perspectives Weekly, and TD Cowen's Insights.

sources.title: End To End Research Workflow
claims: Research production is decomposed into nine stages: idea generation, data collection, model build/update, draft writing, internal review, publication/distribution, sales/client communication, event follow-up, and compliance/archiving.
Internal review led by the Editor and Supervisory Analyst is the most certain bottleneck, especially during earnings season.
Data collection and SA approval are described as permanent bottlenecks in the workflow.

sources.title: Morning Call
claims: The morning call is described as the daily heartbeat of sell-side research, typically starting around 7:30am U.S. Eastern Time.
Coverage analysts pitch initiations, rating changes, and deep dives in roughly 5-10 minutes each.
After the call, the sales team delivers 30-second pitches to clients, and interested clients' orders are handed off to sales traders.

sources.title: Earnings Season Cadence
claims: Earnings season occurs quarterly: January-February for Q4, April-May for Q1, July-August for Q2, and October-November for Q3.
Flash notes must be published within 60-90 minutes of an earnings release.
The season requires 70-80 hour intensive work weeks and 24-hour production coverage.

sources.title: Global Ib Research Platform
claims: Global investment banks such as Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, UBS, Barclays, and Deutsche Bank employ 500-1,000+ research staff across New York, London, and Hong Kong.
Goldman Sachs GIR allocates 62% of staff to Equity Research, 13% to Macro Research, and 25% to Specialized Teams.
JPMorgan has 920 research professionals in 26 countries covering 3,600 equities and 1,100 credits.
This organizational type is the only one that fully develops all 16 Role Families.

sources.title: Full Service Brokerage Research
claims: Full-service brokers such as Nomura, BNP Paribas Exane, and Daiwa Securities provide integrated research, sales, trading, and investment banking with 100-500 research staff.
Nomura Research Institute spun off from Nomura's research department in 1965 and is now an independent consulting and IT solutions entity.
BNP Paribas has formalized the Information Barrier Manager role and Barrier Crossing procedures.

sources.title: Independent Research House
claims: Independent research houses such as Bernstein/Autonomous and Redburn Partners maintain a research-only model to prevent investment banking conflicts.
Bernstein/Autonomous has 750+ employees and dual headquarters in New York and London.
The research-only structure naturally satisfies FINRA Rule 2241's separation requirement between investment banking and research.

sources.title: Sector Specialist Boutique
claims: Evercore ISI was ranked #1 in the Extel All-America Survey from 2022 to 2025, and Ed Hyman's Economics team has held the #1 ranking for 44 consecutive years.
Wolfe Research, founded in 2008, has 250 employees, 28 lead analysts, and covers 575 stocks.
Boutiques have an advantage in relationship-intensive functions such as corporate access, expert networks, and alternative data integration.

sources.title: Lean Regional Broker
claims: CLSA operates 15 Asia-Pacific offices, covers 178 listed companies in China, and its China Forum draws 460+ institutional investors and 90+ Chinese companies.
Among 42 Korean securities firms, 26 recorded a 0% sell ratio, contrasting with J.P. Morgan Seoul branch's 51.3% sell ratio.
Regional brokers face a double squeeze between global IB scale/technology and boutique specialization/agility.

sources.title: Hidden Research Infrastructure
claims: Non-analyst roles in data, technology, operations, editing, compliance, and distribution can account for 50-60% of total research FTE at large investment banks.
Client-visible output quality is directly proportional to the health of invisible infrastructure.
Outsourcing can reduce staffing costs by 50%+ and time-to-market by 30%, but cannot replace regulatory mandatory functions such as Supervisory Analyst approval.

sources.title: Core Research Roles
claims: Ten roles are identified as highest leverage: Coverage Analyst, Research Associate, Equity Strategist, Macro Economist, Quant Researcher, Credit Analyst, Research Salesperson, Editor/SA, ESG Analyst, and Research Technologist.
Coverage Analyst, Strategist, Economist, and Credit Analyst are client-facing idea producers with very high replacement difficulty due to relationship assets.
Editor/SA and Research Technologist are invisible but govern research quality, compliance, and digital transformation.

sources.title: Research Production Bottlenecks
claims: The top five bottlenecks are SA approval, new model build, alternative data cleansing, multimedia production, and ESG regulatory response.
SA approval is hardest to alleviate because FINRA Rule 2241 mandates it and the supply of Series 16-qualified staff is inflexible.
Data cleansing and multimedia production are relatively easier to mitigate through technology investment and organizational design.

sources.title: Automation Human Judgment
claims: Easily automated tasks include formatting/templates, data collection/extraction, translation/localization, basic chart creation, and CRM updates.
Human judgment remains critical for investment recommendations, management meeting evaluation, key assumption setting, regulatory judgment, and ESG financial materiality assessment.
Automation and human judgment are complementary rather than substitutive.

sources.title: Organizational Design Principles
claims: Scale and specialization are non-linear; Global IBs have all 16 Role Families but face coordination costs and relationship dilution.
Hidden infrastructure roles account for 50-60% of research FTE at large IBs and should be treated as core value-chain infrastructure.
SA approval and data cleansing are permanent bottlenecks that must be optimized at the process level.
The boundary between automation and human judgment must be managed dynamically.
Three-party attribution tension among analyst, sales, and trading should be managed constructively through broker vote and multi-faceted evaluation.

sources.title: Future Structural Transitions
claims: Quant-Fundamental Convergence is making Python/SQL data analysis essential for bottom-up analysts and spreading T-shaped skill expectations.
The Three-Layer ESG Model, combining centralized data, sector-embedded integration, and client-facing advisory, is becoming an industry standard.
AI automation is creating a Research Production Paradox: automation reduces manual burden but shifts work to higher-level analysis, raising barriers for junior roles.
Boutique-Global Polarization is squeezing mid-tier regional brokers and independent houses; the market may consolidate into 5-7 global IBs and 10-15 boutiques/independents.
